words.definitionEn: a person who operates equipment or a machine
words.exampleEn: a computer/machine operator

words.definitionEn: a person that you are playing or fighting against in a game, competition, argument, etc.
words.exampleEn: a political opponent

words.definitionEn: to disagree strongly with somebody’s plan, policy, etc. and try to change it or prevent it from succeeding
words.exampleEn: oppose somebody/something, This party would bitterly oppose the re-introduction of the death penalty.

words.definitionEn: disagreeing strongly with something and trying to stop it
words.exampleEn: opposed to something, He was strongly opposed to modernism in art.

words.definitionEn: the act of strongly disagreeing with somebody/something, especially with the aim of preventing something from happening
words.exampleEn: Opposition came primarily from students.

words.definitionEn: expecting good things to happen or something to be successful; showing this feeling
words.exampleEn: We are now taking a more optimistic view.

words.definitionEn: a large group of people who play various musical instruments together, led by a conductor
words.exampleEn: She plays the flute in the school orchestra.

words.definitionEn: a part of the body that has a particular purpose, such as the heart or the brain; part of a plant with a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: the internal organs

words.definitionEn: produced or practised without using artificial chemicals
words.exampleEn: organic cheese/vegetables/wine, etc.

words.definitionEn: the point from which something starts; the cause of something
words.exampleEn: origin of something, the origins of life on earth

words.definitionEn: used to state what the result would be if something did not happen or if the situation were different
words.exampleEn: My parents lent me the money. Otherwise, I couldn't have afforded the trip.

words.definitionEn: the result or effect of an action or event
words.exampleEn: The likely outcome is a compromise.

words.definitionEn: a document that you send to somebody using email
words.exampleEn: No attachment was included.

words.definitionEn: on the outside of something
words.exampleEn: the outer layers of the skin

words.definitionEn: a set of clothes that you wear together, especially for a particular occasion or purpose
words.exampleEn: She was wearing an expensive new outfit.

words.definitionEn: a description of the main facts or points involved in something
words.exampleEn: outline of something, This is a brief outline of the events.

words.definitionEn: to give a description of the main facts or points involved in something
words.exampleEn: outline something, He outlined his plan to leave St. Petersburg.

words.definitionEn: the amount of something that a person, a machine or an organization produces
words.exampleEn: Manufacturing output has increased by 8 per cent.

words.definitionEn: extremely good; excellent
words.exampleEn: an outstanding player/achievement/success

words.definitionEn: to succeed in doing or completing something
words.exampleEn: The first part of the plan has been safely accomplished.

words.definitionEn: including all the things or people that are involved in a particular situation; general
words.exampleEn: There will be winners in each of three age groups, and one overall winner.

words.definitionEn: including everything or everyone; in total
words.exampleEn: The company will invest $1.6m overall in new equipment.

words.definitionEn: to succeed in dealing with or controlling a problem that has been preventing you from achieving something
words.exampleEn: She overcame injury to win the Olympic gold medal.

words.definitionEn: during or for the night
words.exampleEn: We stayed overnight in London after the theatre.

words.definitionEn: connected with foreign countries, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
words.exampleEn: The firm is expanding into overseas markets.

words.definitionEn: to or in a foreign country, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
words.exampleEn: to live/work/go overseas

words.definitionEn: to have to pay somebody for something that you have already received or return money that you have borrowed
words.exampleEn: owe somebody something, She still owes her father £3 000.

words.definitionEn: the fact of owning something
words.exampleEn: a growth in home ownership

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Oxygen is a gas that is present in air and water and is necessary for people, animals and plants to live.
words.exampleEn: The patient didn't seem to be getting enough oxygen.

words.definitionEn: an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult, often with no success
words.exampleEn: I passed my driving test at the first attempt.

words.definitionEn: the speed at which somebody/something walks, runs or moves
words.exampleEn: at a… pace, to set off at a steady/gentle/leisurely pace

words.definitionEn: to walk up and down in a small area many times, especially because you are feeling nervous or angry
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., She paced up and down outside the room.

words.definitionEn: to put something into a box, bag, etc. to be sold or transported
words.exampleEn: package something, packaged food/goods

words.definitionEn: a small container made of paper or card in which goods are packed for selling
words.exampleEn: a packet of biscuits/cigarettes/crisps

words.definitionEn: to make an effort or try to do something, especially something difficult
words.exampleEn: attempt to do something, I will attempt to answer all your questions.

words.definitionEn: a straight tree with a mass of long leaves at the top, growing in tropical countries. There are several types of palm tree, some of which produce fruit.
words.exampleEn: a date palm

words.definitionEn: a square or rectangular piece of wood, glass or metal that forms part of a larger surface such as a door or wall
words.exampleEn: One of the glass panels in the front door was cracked.

words.definitionEn: a sudden feeling of great fear that cannot be controlled and prevents you from thinking clearly
words.exampleEn: a moment of panic

words.definitionEn: a public celebration of a special day or event, usually with bands in the streets and decorated vehicles
words.exampleEn: the Lord Mayor’s parade

words.definitionEn: two or more lines that are parallel to each other are the same distance apart at every point
words.exampleEn: parallel lines

words.definitionEn: a person, a situation, an event, etc. that is very similar to another, especially one in a different place or time
words.exampleEn: These ideas have parallels in Freud's thought too.

words.definitionEn: the group of people who are elected to make and change the laws of a country
words.exampleEn: The German parliament is called the ‘Bundestag’.

words.definitionEn: for part of the day or week in which people work
words.exampleEn: She's looking for a part-time job.

words.definitionEn: a person who is taking part in an activity or event
words.exampleEn: The average age of study participants was 48 years.

words.definitionEn: the act of taking part in an activity or event
words.exampleEn: a show with lots of audience participation

words.definitionEn: to some extent; not completely
words.exampleEn: Some people are unwilling to attend the classes partly because of the cost involved.

words.definitionEn: the state of being a partner in business
words.exampleEn: to be in/to go into partnership

words.definitionEn: a long narrow area with walls on either side that connects one room or place with another
words.exampleEn: A dark narrow passage led to the main hall.

words.definitionEn: having or showing strong feelings of sexual love or of anger, etc.
words.exampleEn: to have a passionate nature

words.definitionEn: a series of letters, numbers, etc. that you must type into a computer or computer system in order to be able to use it
words.exampleEn: Enter a username and password to get into the system.

words.definitionEn: the ability to stay calm and accept a delay or something annoying without complaining
words.exampleEn: She has little patience with (= will not accept or consider) such views.

words.definitionEn: able to wait for a long time or accept annoying behaviour or difficulties without becoming angry
words.exampleEn: You'll just have to be patient and wait till I'm finished.

words.definitionEn: a period of time during which somebody stops talking or stops what they are doing
words.exampleEn: There was a long pause before she answered.

words.definitionEn: to stop talking or doing something for a short time before continuing
words.exampleEn: Anita paused for a moment, then said: ‘All right’.

words.definitionEn: a person who is the same age or who has the same social status as you
words.exampleEn: She enjoys the respect of her peers.

words.definitionEn: a punishment for breaking a law, rule or contract
words.exampleEn: to impose a penalty

words.definitionEn: to understand or think of somebody/something in a particular way
words.exampleEn: perceive somebody/something/yourself (as something), This discovery was perceived as a major breakthrough.

words.definitionEn: an idea, a belief or an image you have as a result of how you see or understand something
words.exampleEn: a campaign to change public perception of the police

words.definitionEn: lasting for a long time or for all time in the future; existing all the time
words.exampleEn: She was unable to find a permanent job.

words.definitionEn: in a way that lasts for a long time or for all time in the future; in a way that exists all the time
words.exampleEn: The stroke left his right side permanently damaged.

words.definitionEn: an official document that gives somebody the right to do something, especially for a limited period of time
words.exampleEn: a parking/building permit

words.definitionEn: to allow somebody to do something or to allow something to happen
words.exampleEn: be permitted, Mobile phones are not permitted in the examination room.

words.definitionEn: a particular attitude towards something; a way of thinking about something
words.exampleEn: a global perspective

words.definitionEn: a stage in a process of change or development
words.exampleEn: during the initial/final phase

words.definitionEn: a fact or an event in nature or society, especially one that is not fully understood
words.exampleEn: cultural/natural phenomena

words.definitionEn: the study of the nature and meaning of the universe and of human life
words.exampleEn: philosophy of something, the philosophy of science

words.definitionEn: a public sale in which things are sold to the person who offers the most money for them
words.exampleEn: an auction of paintings

words.definitionEn: an act of choosing something
words.exampleEn: Take your pick (= choose).

words.definitionEn: to imagine somebody/something; to create an image of somebody/something in your mind
words.exampleEn: picture somebody/something, I could picture the scene clearly.

words.definitionEn: a number of things that have been placed on top of each other
words.exampleEn: pile of something, a pile of clothes/paper

words.definitionEn: to put things one on top of another; to form a pile
words.exampleEn: pile something, She piled the boxes one on top of the other.

words.definitionEn: a small flat round piece of medicine that you swallow whole, without biting it
words.exampleEn: a vitamin pill

words.definitionEn: connected with sound that is recorded
words.exampleEn: audio and video recordings/files/clips

words.definitionEn: an area of ground specially prepared and marked for playing a sports game
words.exampleEn: a football pitch

words.definitionEn: used to show that you are disappointed about something
words.exampleEn: a pity (that…), It's a pity that you can't stay longer.

words.definitionEn: the act of finding somebody a suitable job or place to live
words.exampleEn: a job placement service

words.definitionEn: not decorated or complicated; simple
words.exampleEn: a plain but elegant dress

words.definitionEn: to make a secret plan to harm somebody, especially a government or its leader
words.exampleEn: plot with somebody, It is claimed he plotted with the country's enemies.

words.definitionEn: used after a number to show that the real number or amount is more than the one mentioned
words.exampleEn: The work will cost £10 000 plus.

words.definitionEn: used to add more information
words.exampleEn: I've got too much on at work. Plus my father is not well.

words.definitionEn: an advantage; a good thing
words.exampleEn: Knowledge of French is a plus in her job.

words.definitionEn: having a sharp end
words.exampleEn: a pointed chin

words.definitionEn: the state of being liked, enjoyed or supported by a large number of people
words.exampleEn: the increasing popularity of cycling

words.definitionEn: one part of something larger
words.exampleEn: a substantial/significant portion of the population

words.definitionEn: to create a threat, problem, etc. that has to be dealt with
words.exampleEn: to pose a threat/risk/challenge/danger

words.definitionEn: to put somebody/something in a particular position
words.exampleEn: position somebody/something/yourself + adv./prep., She quickly positioned herself behind the desk.

words.definitionEn: a good or useful quality or aspect
words.exampleEn: What are the positives and negatives of going this route?

words.definitionEn: to have or own something
words.exampleEn: He was charged with possessing a shotgun without a licence.

words.definitionEn: having controls that work without needing a person to operate them
words.exampleEn: automatic doors

words.definitionEn: without needing a person to operate controls
words.exampleEn: The heating switches off automatically.

words.definitionEn: that can develop into something or be developed in the future
words.exampleEn: potential customers/buyers/investors/clients

words.definitionEn: the possibility of something happening or being developed or used
words.exampleEn: potential for something, the potential for change

words.definitionEn: used to say that something may develop into something
words.exampleEn: a potentially dangerous situation

words.definitionEn: to supply a machine or vehicle with the energy that makes it work
words.exampleEn: be powered (by something), The aircraft is powered by a jet engine.

words.definitionEn: words that show that you approve of and admire somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The team coach singled out two players for special praise.

words.definitionEn: to say that you approve of and admire somebody/something
words.exampleEn: praise somebody/something, She praised his cooking.

words.definitionEn: to happen before something or come before something/somebody in order
words.exampleEn: the years preceding the war

words.definitionEn: rare and worth a lot of money
words.exampleEn: a precious vase

words.definitionEn: clear and accurate
words.exampleEn: precise details/instructions/measurements

words.definitionEn: exactly
words.exampleEn: They look precisely the same to me.

words.definitionEn: if something is predictable, you know in advance that it will happen or what it will be like
words.exampleEn: a predictable result

words.definitionEn: a greater interest in or desire for somebody/something than somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: It's a matter of personal preference.

words.definitionEn: having a baby or young animal developing inside her/its body
words.exampleEn: My wife is pregnant.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of getting ready for something or making something ready
words.exampleEn: food preparation

words.definitionEn: the fact of being in a particular place
words.exampleEn: He hardly seemed to notice my presence.

words.definitionEn: to keep a particular quality, feature, etc.; to make sure that something is kept
words.exampleEn: He was anxious to preserve his reputation.

words.definitionEn: to fix the price of something at a particular level
words.exampleEn: be priced + adv./prep., The main courses are all reasonably priced.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of being pleased or satisfied that you get when you or people who are connected with you have done something well or own something that other people admire
words.exampleEn: The sight of her son graduating filled her with pride.

words.definitionEn: mainly
words.exampleEn: a course designed primarily for specialists

words.definitionEn: main; most important; basic
words.exampleEn: My prime concern is to protect my property.

words.definitionEn: most important; main
words.exampleEn: The principal reason for this omission is lack of time.

words.definitionEn: a moral rule or a strong belief that influences your actions
words.exampleEn: He has high moral principles.

words.definitionEn: letters, words, numbers, etc. that have been printed onto paper
words.exampleEn: The tiny print was hard to read without my glasses.

words.definitionEn: happening or existing before something else or before a particular time
words.exampleEn: Although not essential, some prior knowledge of statistics is desirable.

words.definitionEn: something that you think is more important than other things and should be dealt with first
words.exampleEn: a high/low priority

words.definitionEn: the state of being alone and not watched or interrupted by other people
words.exampleEn: She was longing for some peace and privacy.

words.definitionEn: how likely something is to happen
words.exampleEn: The probability is that prices will rise rapidly.

words.definitionEn: likely to happen, to exist or to be true
words.exampleEn: the probable cause/explanation/outcome

words.definitionEn: a way of doing something, especially the usual or correct way
words.exampleEn: emergency/safety/disciplinary procedures

words.definitionEn: to continue doing something that has already been started; to continue being done
words.exampleEn: We're not sure whether we still want to proceed with the sale.

words.definitionEn: things that have been made or grown, especially things connected with farming
words.exampleEn: The market is full of farm produce.

words.definitionEn: a person who does a job that needs special training and a high level of education
words.exampleEn: You need a professional to sort out your finances.

words.definitionEn: knowing something; knowing that something exists and is important
words.exampleEn: awareness of something, an awareness of the importance of eating a healthy diet

words.definitionEn: the process of writing and testing computer programs
words.exampleEn: programming languages

words.definitionEn: to improve or develop over a period of time; to make progress
words.exampleEn: The course allows students to progress at their own speed.

words.definitionEn: in favour of new ideas, modern methods and change
words.exampleEn: progressive schools

words.definitionEn: to stop something from being done or used especially by law
words.exampleEn: prohibit something, a law prohibiting the sale of alcohol

words.definitionEn: to estimate what the size, cost or amount of something will be in the future based on what is happening now
words.exampleEn: be projected, A growth rate of 4 per cent is projected for next year.

words.definitionEn: showing signs of being good or successful
words.exampleEn: He was voted the most promising new actor for his part in the movie.

words.definitionEn: a move to a more important job or rank in a company or an organization
words.exampleEn: The new job is a promotion for him.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody decide to do something; to cause something to happen
words.exampleEn: prompt something, The discovery of the bomb prompted an increase in security.

words.definitionEn: information, documents, etc. that show that something is true
words.exampleEn: conclusive/definitive/scientific proof

words.definitionEn: a part or share of a whole
words.exampleEn: Water covers a large proportion of the earth's surface.
